Document:
# Animal Husbandry
[color=#9fed58]Animal Husbandry[/color] is the new era of taking care of your animals and getting new ones. No longer is there any reliance on [color=cyan]Cargo[/color] needed! Kind of.
## The Basics
All mobs are split into three distinct Genders. Your Male, your female and your agender. Sometimes Central will be nice and supply you with a couple of animals at the start of a shift but more than likely you're going to have to visit [color=cyan]Cargo[/color] to get yourself a crate. They sell crates containing a breedable pair of each animal though so it's not too much of a hastle. It can actually be cheaper to buy the pairing crates too!
Animals are able to breed based on several different factors. - Their Genders (Females & Males breed together. Agender breed together.) - Hunger - Thirst - Health
So it's important to make sure your animals are paired up right as well as making sure they're nice and healthy!
When a male or agender animal has met their needs, then will then occasionally head out and look for a suitable mate, who also needs to be healthy! At which point the pair will breed and now you have a pregnant animal! Now all you need to do is wait for them to give birth, maybe feed them again as well. Breeding is tiring!
## So what happens when the animal is born?
Animals come in one of two types of birth. [color=#9fed58]Instant[/color] or [color=#9fed58]Incubated.[/color]
[color=#9fed58]Instant[/color] births are the kind you see in mammals where the animal is born directly. For instance a Cow will give birth to a Calf who is ready to (try) walking the Earth now that it is born.
[color=#9fed58]Incubated[/color] births are the kind you see in birds, fish, etc. These animals will lay eggs that need to be hatched in a good environment, this is why we have the [color=#9fed58]Incubator![/color] A handy little device you can build yourself with only a few resources. When powered you can place an egg within an [color=#9fed58]Incubator[/color] to begin the hatching process. This will take a little bit of patience but thankfully animals that lay eggs don't stay pregnant as long as those that don't. After a while though the egg in the [color=#9fed58]Incubator[/color] will hatch leaving you with a wonderful little baby to take care of!
Once your animals are born the only things you need to do are to once again keep them well fed and healthy, they'll grow up over time until they're ready to continue the cycle of life!
## Your Animals Diet and You!
Now that we understand how we take care of our animals as well as how to get them breeding, the question becomes what to even feed them.
Animals are a bit more difficult with their diets than most, depending on the animal of course. Animals such as Cows, Pigs, Chickens and Goats can only eat fruits, vegetables and uh... Other plant based things? Maybe they can help with those weeds taking over [color=#9fed58]Botany[/color] sometimes...
If you're looking at your herd of 3 bulls and 20 cows with dread at the thought of hand feeding them though, don't worry. Animals will happily seek out any food you leave out that they can actually eat. So just make sure they have a good stock in their pen and you should be set.
